title: Koeleria sibirensis
intro: Koeleria sibirensis, also known as Siberian Koeleria, is a type of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Siberia and Mongolia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Asian medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems. Koeleria sibirensis contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in traditional medicine in the form of tinctures and powders.
taxonomy: Koeleria sibirensis is classified as a species of grass within the genus Koeleria and family Poaceae. It is a member of the subfamily Pooideae. Koeleria sibirensis further belongs to the order Poales. The species is native to the mountainous regions of Siberia. The classification of Koeleria sibirensis places it within the kingdom Plantae.
